raw_consumer_statement: Made an online purchase for television service at a motel. Cancelled before stated date to avoid recurring charges. Noticed charges not made by myself on card. Filed dispute. Card was cancelled and replaced. Next month, charges were again made on new account. Again, filed dispute and card replaced. Third month, same thing happened. Wash, rinse and repeat. This time, I contacted service provider, and it was discovered that there was indeed fraudulent charges being made, and stated they would stop it. Why was card company, after reaching a dispute resolution in my favor, still charging my card? My thinking it was easier to just resolve dispute, as opposed to actually researching and finding guilty party. Meanwhile I keep getting recurring charges that I keep disputing. I did inform agent that I would cancel card if it happened one more time. I am curious to see what will happen in XXXX. This is after service company stated they will stop charges, as seen as a fraud purchase. This company is the streaming company XXXX. I do not have any contracts through XXXX. They can verify this. But CitiBank keeps reposting my account after cancelling account and settling dispute in my favor. Please advise. Thank you.
